faqs.q: Which visa route applies to me?
faqs.a: We auto-detect it from each pilgrim’s passport: EU/EEA passports use the Saudi e-Visa; Pakistani and other passports route through a MoRA-licensed agent channel. Mixed groups are supported — each pilgrim sees their own route.

faqs.q: What currency am I charged in?
faqs.a: Always EUR. We also show an indicative PKR figure at today’s rate (1 € = ₨310.8) for reference, but the charged amount is never silently converted.

faqs.q: Can I book for a group?
faqs.a: Yes — up to 49 pilgrims in one booking, with per-pilgrim documents and visa routing. Travel agents have a dedicated portal with wallet, credit and markups.

faqs.q: How do I track my booking?
faqs.a: Every booking gets a BRN with a live status timeline (booked → documents → visa → travel → return) and a QR digital pass.

faqs.q: Do you offer Hajj and general travel, not just Umrah?
faqs.a: Yes. Alongside Umrah and Ziyarat, AUJ books Ministry-regulated Hajj and ordinary leisure travel — hotels and flights — through the same cart. The general-travel leg has no Saudi dependency.

faqs.q: Can travel agents sell through AUJ?
faqs.a: Yes. The agent portal gives your agency a wallet, a credit limit, configurable markups, multi-pilgrim booking and reconciling statements. Register, and an administrator approves your account.

testimonialQuotes: The visa route was clear from the first screen — no surprises. Everything in one cart.
Paid in EUR, saw the PKR figure for my family back home. Beautifully simple.
Live BRN and visa tracking gave my parents real peace of mind.

footer.tagline: Making your pilgrimage simple, protected and spiritually focused — with dedicated support at every step.
footer.protectionDesc: Your bookings are financially protected — a consumer security certificate is issued with every package.
footer.connectSub: Seasonal deals and visa updates — no spam.
footer.legal: AUJ is a licensed EU tour operator and acts as agent for the service provider on each booking; your contract is with that provider except where stated otherwise. Eligible packages are covered by an insolvency-protection certificate under the EU Package Travel Directive, issued before you pay. See your booking conditions for the protection that applies.

prayer.title: Prayer times
prayer.sub: Today, by the official Umm al-Qura method.
prayer.jamaatNote: Iqamah (jamaat) is a few minutes after the adhan — confirm at your mosque.
prayer.fallback: Times unavailable right now — check the Nusuk app or your nearest mosque.

day.title: Plan your day around the prayers
items: Reach the Haram before Fajr and pray in congregation.
Stay for dhikr and Qur’an after Fajr until sunrise — great reward.
Return well before Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib and Isha to beat the crowds.
Use the quieter hours for tawaf, ziyarah and personal dua.
Sleep early so Tahajjud and Fajr come easily.

safety.title: Safety & emergencies
safety.asefny: Install the Saudi Red Crescent “Asefny” app — it shares your GPS and finds the nearest hospital.
safety.hospitalNote: Keep your hotel name & address and your group leader’s number handy.

transport.title: Getting around
transport.sub: Between the two holy cities and the airports.
items: Makkah ↔ Madinah on the Haramain high-speed train — about 2–2.5 hours.
The train also serves Jeddah and King Abdulaziz Airport (KAIA).
Bus (SAPTCO) and private car are slower alternatives (about 5–6 hours).
Book the free Rawdah (Riyadh ul-Jannah) permit in the Nusuk app before Madinah.

tips.title: Helpful tips
items: Get a local SIM/eSIM on arrival for maps and calls.
Label your sandals or use the mosque shoe racks/bags.
Carry a small Zamzam bottle and stay hydrated.
Electric carts are available at both mosques for the elderly and limited mobility.
Use the Nusuk app for permits and e-services.
